current_source_anchors.implication: Trustworthy AI posture needs governed risk management, measurement, and claim controls.
Generative-AI decision support needs explicit controls for hallucination, misuse, transparency, and security risks.
AI systems should be robust, secure, safe, accountable, transparent, and human-centered.
Forecasting accuracy claims need probabilistic judgment, aggregation, and empirical testing against real events.

current_source_anchors.source: Metaculus track record and calibration evidence
current_source_anchors.implication: Forecast platforms compete on scored resolved questions, calibration curves, and transparent track records.
AI forecasting claims need dynamic, continuously updated benchmark comparisons rather than static fixtures alone.
AI forecasting agents should be evaluated on real future outcomes and compared against human, community, pro-forecaster, and model baselines.
Buyer-facing forecasting services sell strategic uncertainty reduction, custom forecasts, monitoring, and training.
Enterprise substitutes emphasize operational decision workflows, ontology grounding, automations, and human approval.
Risk-intelligence buyers expect real-time signal detection, alerting, public-data coverage, and operational response workflows.

current_source_anchors.source: World Economic Forum Global Risks Report 2026
current_source_anchors.implication: Geoeconomic confrontation, conflict, polarization, misinformation, and AI risk provide current buyer-pain context.
Enterprise/public-sector trust claims need current cybersecurity governance, risk, and control evidence.

current_source_anchors.source: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications
current_source_anchors.implication: LLM apps need prompt-injection, output-handling, data, supply-chain, and agent/tool security proof.
Security posture should shift customer risk back to the software maker through secure defaults and evidence.

loopholes.loophole: Downstream prediction, buyer, enterprise, hosted, payment, AI-action, and claim-language evidence cannot be upgraded until the owner approval register is reviewed and approved.
loopholes.buyer_impact: Without explicit owner approvals, the product can accidentally convert templates, local checks, or unreviewed rows into buyer-facing claims.
loopholes.remediation: Owner reviews every required approval row, acknowledges claim boundaries, approves or rejects downstream evidence execution, and reruns the owner approval register validator before downstream gates.
loopholes.proof_to_close: Owner approval validation reports all required approvals ready for downstream evidence and zero row errors.

loopholes.loophole: The product can describe forecasting mechanics, but it cannot claim accurate or world-class predictions without real resolved forecast rows, comparable baselines, leakage review, and scoring review.
loopholes.buyer_impact: Strategic buyers will reject prediction-superiority claims unless the track record is scored, calibrated, and benchmarked.
loopholes.remediation: Run the resolved-forecast export, validate baselines, complete leakage review, compare Brier/log-loss/calibration metrics, and keep claims at mechanics-only until the gate passes.
loopholes.proof_to_close: Forecast execution readiness reports real resolved outcomes and baselines; leakage and scoring validators allow accuracy claims.

loopholes.loophole: Market research identifies plausible buyer pain, but willingness-to-pay is still unproven without completed discovery calls, qualified follow-ups, paid pilots, LOIs, or procurement signals.
loopholes.buyer_impact: Without buyer evidence, the strategy is desk-validated rather than sellability-validated.
loopholes.remediation: Execute the 10-call buyer validation slate across the five niches and rerun buyer evidence/proof gates.
loopholes.proof_to_close: Buyer proof gate allows buyer-validated and willingness-to-pay claims.

loopholes.loophole: The differentiated wedge is plausible, but it has not yet beaten substitutes in buyer conversations or procurement comparison.
loopholes.evidence: competitive_positioning_validation_ready_pilot_only_not_buyer_validated; defensible_wedge=true; substitution_protocol_ready=true; substitution_test_rows=30; substitution_evidence_ready=false; real_substitution_interactions=0; buyer_validated=false; substitute_categories=0/0.
loopholes.buyer_impact: Buyers may view the app as a lightweight substitute for Palantir-style decision workflows, Dataminr-style risk alerting, Metaculus/Good Judgment forecasting, or internal analyst workflows.
loopholes.remediation: Execute the buyer substitution test sheet and record current tool, budget owner, replacement barrier, must-have proof, and paid pilot threshold.
loopholes.proof_to_close: Buyer evidence shows the app wins a specific pilot wedge against named substitutes.
loopholes.claim_boundary: defensible competitive wedge allowed; replacement/parity claims blocked

loopholes.loophole: Local route proof is useful, but hosted-live claims and Browser-verified production behavior remain blocked by hosted access and smoke execution.
loopholes.buyer_impact: Enterprise buyers and pilot sponsors need proof that the live system works in the actual deployed environment.
loopholes.remediation: Resolve project visibility/management access, Stripe proof values, and deploy binding, then run hosted Browser/Playwright smoke tests.
loopholes.proof_to_close: Hosted smoke execution readiness and hosted operational proof validators both pass with live evidence.

loopholes.loophole: Enterprise/public-sector positioning is attractive, but trust proof is incomplete without procurement documents, RLS execution, hosted access proof, and approved AI action controls.
loopholes.buyer_impact: Procurement teams will treat the product as an interesting pilot, not enterprise-ready infrastructure.
loopholes.remediation: Approve trust documents, run RLS proof cases, finish hosted security proof, and keep high-impact AI actions under human review.
loopholes.proof_to_close: Enterprise trust execution readiness allows enterprise/public-sector trust claims.

loopholes.loophole: Local LLM red-team and policy artifacts are not enough for hosted AI governance claims.
loopholes.buyer_impact: AI governance buyers will ask whether prompt/tool risks are controlled in the deployed product, not only in local fixtures.
loopholes.remediation: Run hosted LLM security smoke checks and keep refusal, provenance, tool/action, and human-review gates documented.
loopholes.proof_to_close: Hosted LLM/security evidence is present and claim consistency remains clean.

loopholes.loophole: The original worldwide/world-class ambition is too broad for the current evidence; the sellable scope should stay focused on five pilot niches.
loopholes.buyer_impact: Over-broad claims weaken credibility versus mature enterprise intelligence and forecasting vendors.
loopholes.remediation: Use the five-niche pilot narrative and prohibit world-class/accurate-prediction copy until evidence gates pass.
loopholes.proof_to_close: 95 percent confidence and goal-completion audit pass with no unsupported claims.

loopholes.loophole: Sample fixture scoring can prove validator mechanics, but it cannot prove predictive accuracy.
loopholes.buyer_impact: A technically sophisticated buyer will distinguish fixture correctness from real forecasting skill.
loopholes.remediation: Use sample fixtures only for harness checks; require real resolved events before score claims.
loopholes.proof_to_close: Scoring validation uses real rows and benchmark comparisons.
loopholes.claim_boundary: fixture proof is not accuracy proof

loopholes.loophole: The claim surface is currently clean, but every generated artifact or landing copy change can reintroduce unsupported world-class, hosted-live, buyer-validated, or accuracy claims.
loopholes.buyer_impact: Clean language protects credibility while evidence is still pilot-only.
loopholes.remediation: Rerun claim consistency after every launch-readiness, pricing, landing, or outreach language change.
loopholes.proof_to_close: Claim consistency remains clean after final buyer-facing copy is generated.

remediation_loop.action: Owner reviews and approves the consolidated approval register before downstream prediction, buyer, enterprise, hosted, payment, AI-action, and claim-language proof runs.
Collect owner-approved resolved forecasts and baselines, then rerun leakage, scoring, science, confidence, and completion gates.
Execute the 10-call buyer validation slate and record willingness-to-pay/substitution evidence.
Approve procurement documents, run RLS proof, and complete hosted LLM/security evidence.
Unblock project privileges and execute hosted Browser/Playwright smoke tests.
Keep all public copy constrained to pilot-only governed decision support until the completion audit passes.

decision: Keep the strategy at pilot-only. The strongest current marketable wedge is governed strategic-intelligence decision support with calibration-aware forecasting mechanics, not proven world-class prediction accuracy.
proof_boundary: This validator consolidates loopholes and fixes. It does not create buyer calls, hosted proof, enterprise approvals, or real prediction outcomes.
